{{Quote|They arrested Maxa Jandovar on Morvogodine. She died in custody.|Kardue'sai'Malloc|<ref name="Empire Blues">{{StoryCite|story=Empire Blues: The Devaronian's Tale|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}}</ref>}}
'''Morvogodine''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] crawling with life that was located in the [[Calaron sector/Legends|Calaron sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] occupied Morvogodine during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] and hosted a notoriously vicious [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] [[Morvogodine stormtrooper training facility|training facility]] on the planet. Shortly before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the Empire arrested famous [[Music/Legends|musician]] [[Maxa Jandovar]] on Morvogodine to suppress her [[Politics/Legends|politically]] outspoken [[Artist/Legends|artistic]] endeavors.

==Description==
The [[Planet/Legends|planet]] Morvogodine, part of the [[Calaron sector/Legends|Calaron sector's]]<ref name="CSWE-Morvogodine" /> [[Morvogodine system/Legends|Morvogodine system]], was located in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Atlas" /> Morvogodine was a world teeming with life—its varying conditions and plentiful [[Star/Legends|sunlight]] produced many different [[Plant/Legends|plant]] and animal [[Species/Legends|species]]. The planet's surface features included landmasses and expanses of [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]].<ref name="Rebellion" />

==History==
{{Quote|I was on Tatooine when I got the news, getting ready to head to Morvogodine.|Kardue'sai'Malloc|<ref name="Empire Blues" />}}
[[File:StrengthofCommand-KaynSomosVillainPack.png|thumb|left|275px|Stormtrooper trainees on Morvogodine]]
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Morvogodine fell under occupation by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="CSWE-Morvogodine" /> Following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="Date">The ''[[Kayn Somos Villain Pack]]'' is part of the ''[[Star Wars: Imperial Assault]]'' miniatures [[Roleplaying game|game]], which begins in the aftermath of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] according to {{FantasyFlightGames|url=en/news/2014/8/15/imperial-assault|text=Imperial Assault}}.</ref> the planet was the site of an especially vicious [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] [[Morvogodine stormtrooper training facility|training facility]] run by [[Stormtrooper Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Kayn Somos/Legends|Kayn Somos]], who sent its students on "peacekeeping" missions as a final test.<ref name="Kayn Somos" />

The famed [[vandfill]]ist [[Artist/Legends|artist]] [[Maxa Jandovar]]<ref name="CSWE-Jandovar">{{CSWECite|II|137|Jandovar, Maxa}}</ref> included Morvogodine as part of her ongoing informal [[Music/Legends|musical]] tour in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] preceding<ref name="Empire Blues" /> [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].<ref name="Essential Reader's Companion">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> An outspoken [[Politics/Legends|political]] opponent of the Empire, Jandovar was forced to follow an unannounced touring schedule, lest she reveal her next performance stop and find Imperial forces waiting to seize her. Nevertheless, the Empire arrested Jandovar on Morvogodine, and she later [[Death/Legends|died]] in custody. The [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] [[Kardue'sai'Malloc/Legends|Kardue'sai'Malloc]], who followed Jandovar around [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in an attempt to catch one of her performances, was arranging [[Transport/Legends|transport]] to Morvogodine from the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] to see her when he learned that Jandovar had been captured.<ref name="Empire Blues" />

==Behind the scenes==
The planet Morvogodine was first mentioned—as the site where the Empire arrested artist Maxa Jandovar—in the short story "[[Empire Blues: The Devaronian's Tale]]," written by [[Daniel Keys Moran]] and published in the [[1995]] anthology book ''[[Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina]]''.<ref name="Empire Blues" /> Morvogodine was later one of 200 playable worlds included in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|PC game]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']], which provided the planet's first visual depiction, an image also used to illustrate eleven other planets of similar composition in the game.<ref name="Rebellion" />

===''Star Wars: Rebellion'' cartography===
''Star Wars: Rebellion'' provides [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] travel [[Time/Legends|times]] between each planet in the game. However, in several instances these times are significantly longer than those in other [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] sources, which seems to reflect the ''Star Wars: Rebellion'' hyperspace times as game mechanics rather than actual figures. Morvogodine's intra-[[Sector/Legends|sector]] hyperspace times and distance from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] according to ''Star Wars: Rebellion'' using the game's fastest [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] are as follows. Note that this chart only includes planets that were [[canon]]ically located in the Calaron sector with respect to the Legends continuity, excepting Coruscant.
